- **Four doc-level references** — [docs/RULE-INVENTORY.md](RULE-INVENTORY.md) (the rule's heading + body), [docs/RUNBOOK.md](RUNBOOK.md) (`cd zeemish-v2` example + project-tree diagram), [docs/FOLLOWUPS.md](FOLLOWUPS.md) (two `github.com/zzeeshann/zeemish-v2/commit/…` URLs), and [src/interactive/made-drawer.ts:266](../src/interactive/made-drawer.ts:266) (the user-facing GitHub link to voice-contract.md in the *How this was made* drawer). The made-drawer link is the only reader-visible one; the rest are operator/maintainer-facing.
- **Local git remote** — switched from `https://github.com/zzeeshann/zeemish-v2.git` to `https://github.com/zzeeshann/daylila.git`. Push to the new URL succeeded; CI ([run 25453299907](https://github.com/zzeeshann/daylila/actions/runs/25453299907)) deployed both workers green from the renamed repo.

**What was intentionally NOT renamed, and why.**

- **Cloudflare worker name `zeemish-v2`** (the site worker) and **`zeemish-agents`** (the agents worker, which holds 9 Durable Object classes for the agent team). These names are internal labels — they don't appear on any reader-facing surface, only in the Cloudflare dashboard and in `wrangler.toml`. Renaming them would require either (a) deploying new workers under the new name and migrating all Durable Object instances across, or (b) using `wrangler` migrations with renamed_classes / new worker bindings — both of which carry a real risk of orphaning DO state (per-agent SQLite storage) or breaking the service binding from the site worker to the agents worker. Reward is cosmetic. Risk is non-zero. The 2026-05-06 rebrand entry's third paragraph ("Out of scope by deliberate deferral") already covered this trade-off and the call hasn't changed — what changed was the GitHub repo, which has zero state migration cost because GitHub auto-redirects.
- **R2 bucket `zeemish-audio`** — bound to both workers as `AUDIO_BUCKET`. Renaming would require creating the new bucket, copying every object across (every per-beat MP3 generated since 2026-04-18), updating bindings on both workers, and a brief inconsistency window. Same calculus: cosmetic reward, real risk of audio 404s during the migration window.
- **D1 database `zeemish`** (`f3cdccbf-7cea-4af1-b524-20f6a6fe1dd4`) — the database name is just a label; the binding is by ID. The label has zero functional effect. Leaving.
- **KV namespace IDs** — bound by ID, not name. Same as D1.
- **`package.json` `"name": "zeemish-v2"`** at the repo root and `"zeemish-agents"` in `agents/package.json` — npm package names. Neither package is published to a registry. The name is a label. Parallel to the Cloudflare worker name and left untouched for the same reason.
- **Workers.dev URL `https://zeemish-v2.zzeeshann.workers.dev`** referenced in [agents/src/server.ts:39](../agents/src/server.ts:39) CORS allowlist — this is the workers.dev URL that's a function of the worker name. Stays in lockstep with the worker name.
- **Existing `zeemish-v2` mentions in docs that describe the worker** ([CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) "What was built" section, [docs/RUNBOOK.md](RUNBOOK.md) URL preamble, [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md) staging-environment future-flexibility section, [docs/FOLLOWUPS.md](FOLLOWUPS.md) site-worker note) — these describe the worker by its actual name. They stay accurate as long as the worker name does.

**Current state of `zeemish.io`.** Abandoned. Custom-domain binding removed from the `zeemish-v2` worker. All 4 DNS records on the zone deleted. External resolvers return no record. The domain is registered through Cloudflare for the remainder of its current registration year and will lapse at next renewal unless deliberately reclaimed.

**Operator-visible result.** The reader hits `daylila.com`. The GitHub repo is `zzeeshann/daylila`. The codebase is on `daylila`. The Drafter pushes to `repos/zzeeshann/daylila/contents/...`. Everything reader-facing or operator-facing has the new name. Internal infrastructure (worker names, R2 bucket name, D1 name, KV IDs, npm package names) still uses the old labels. None of those leak — they're internal trivia. The migration is functionally complete.

**What this entry supersedes from 2026-05-06.** The "Deferred to a later phase" section of the 2026-05-06 entry listed: GitHub repo rename, breathing-tools DNS retirement, R2 `zeemish-listens` cleanup. All three are done. The "Out of scope by deliberate deferral" section of that entry — covering Cloudflare worker names, R2 `zeemish-audio`, D1 `zeemish`, KV IDs, npm package names — still stands and is reaffirmed above.

## 2026-05-06: Rebrand Zeemish → Daylila and zeemish.io → daylila.com

The platform is renamed. "Zeemish" becomes "Daylila"; the domain `zeemish.io` becomes `daylila.com`. Brief downtime is acceptable; cleanliness matters more than speed. Every reader-visible surface, every agent prompt, every contract, every chapter of the book, and every living doc is updated in this commit.

**The name.** Daylila comes from the day lily — a flower that opens one fresh bloom every single morning, gone by evening. The metaphor IS the product: one fully-finished daily piece each day, nothing rolled over, nothing held back, nothing edited after the fact. The pipeline ships its bloom and lets it stand. Tomorrow there's another one. The permanence rule (published pieces are immutable) was already the practice; the new name just states it out loud.

**Why now.** The audience is still small. A clean foundation has a price tag and that price is lowest before there's anyone to migrate, before search engines have indexed thousands of URLs, before brand equity is meaningful. Doing it later means migrating readers, redoing SEO, re-verifying email senders, re-rendering social cards already cached at every share. Doing it now means a single commit and a few operator actions on a quiet day.

**Out of scope by deliberate deferral.** Internal worker names (`zeemish-v2`, `zeemish-agents`, `zeemish-api`), the GitHub repo name (`zeemish-v2`), the R2 bucket (`zeemish-audio`), the D1 database (`zeemish`), and KV namespace IDs all stay as-is. They're internal infrastructure with zero reader visibility. Renaming them needs a coordinated GitHub-rename + CI-update + binding-update pass and is its own follow-up. Same for the legacy breathing-tools DNS records (`listens.zeemish.io`, `api.zeemish.io`) — they're leftovers from a defunct earlier product, deferred to a separate cleanup phase.

**Files changed by category:**
- **A. Live-effect config + code** — 7 files: `astro.config.mjs` (canonical site URL), `wrangler.toml` (EMAIL_FROM + Resend comment), `agents/src/server.ts` (CORS allowlist swap, no `zeemish.io` carryover), `src/pages/rss.xml.ts` (fallback URL + feed title), `src/pages/sitemap.xml.ts` (fallback URL), `src/pages/api/auth/magic-link.ts` (sender + subject + body), `src/pages/api/zita/chat.ts` (Zita system prompt brand reference)
- **B. Reader-visible UI** — ~20 files including `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` (10 sites: JSON-LD Organization/Publisher/Author × 3, OG site_name + image alt, RSS link title, header, footer copyright, cleanHeadline regex), `src/layouts/LessonLayout.astro` title template, every page title across `src/pages/*`, `src/lib/constants.ts` comment, `src/interactive/audio-player.ts` MediaSession metadata, plus the OG-image source `scripts/generate-og-image.mjs` (PNG regenerated from updated source). Two sessionStorage key prefixes also bumped (`zeemish-interactive-viewed:*`, `zeemish-interactive-offered:*`) — returning readers will see the dedup re-fire once per interactive, then dedupe correctly going forward.
- **C. Agent prompts** — 15 files: every pipeline agent's prompt + Zita's system prompt + the type / observer / scanner / audio-producer comments. Scanner's RSS User-Agent header switches from `Zeemish/1.0` to `Daylila/1.0` so feed-publisher access logs reflect the new name immediately.
- **C-special. TTS prosody alias** — 3 files: `agents/src/shared/tts-normalize.ts` swaps `Zeemish → Zee-mish` for `Daylila → Day-lila` so ElevenLabs reads the new brand cleanly. `agents/scripts/verify-normalize.mjs` regression cases updated; `agents/scripts/verify-dedup.mjs` comment updated. Verifier passes 20/20.
- **E. Canonical contracts** — all 8 files in `content/`: voice, beat, interactive, audio, curator, audit, fact-check, categoriser. Codegen regenerates `agents/src/shared/generated/contracts.ts` cleanly (zero remaining "Zeemish" mentions in the generated file).
- **D. Living docs** — ~13 files: README, CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE, RUNBOOK, PROJECT-BRIEF, INTERACTIVES, FOLLOWUPS, AGENTS, SCHEMA, zita-design, SESSION_PROTOCOL, INTERACTIVES_STATUS, RULE-INVENTORY. CLAUDE.md gets one new heritage line near the top stating that the platform was originally launched as Zeemish and rebranded on 2026-05-06.
- **F. Book** — all 23 chapter / index files. Chapter 08 file rename (`book/08-zeemish-the-idea.md` → `book/08-daylila-the-idea.md`) plus `book/CONTENTS.md` link update. Inside the chapters, straight find/replace; no chapter rewrite. Book voice and prose preserved.
- **G. DECISIONS.md** — this entry. Existing entries are deliberately preserved with the prior brand name intact — they describe what was true at the time. The file title and the at-top preamble are updated.

**Operator actions required** (these don't ride in this commit; they're configuration on third-party platforms or DNS, and need to happen for the deploy to be functional):

1. **Resend domain verification on `daylila.com`.** Add `resend._domainkey` TXT, `send` MX, and SPF TXT records to the daylila.com DNS zone, then verify in the Resend dashboard. Until that's done the site builds and deploys but **magic-link login fails silently** (Resend rejects the send, the API route swallows the error per its design). This is the highest-priority operator action.
2. **Cloudflare custom-domain bind.** In the dashboard, bind `daylila.com` (apex) and `www.daylila.com` to the `zeemish-v2` worker. Until done, `daylila.com` 404s. Brief downtime is the acceptable cost.
3. **301 redirect rule.** Cloudflare Page Rule (or Bulk Redirect) `zeemish.io/*` → `https://daylila.com/$1` permanent. Carries every external link, every search-result, and every existing RSS subscriber over to the new domain. Without this, RSS subscribers and any inbound link goes dead the moment the worker stops responding on `zeemish.io`.
4. **Cache purge.** Standard step on every deploy (per the existing CLAUDE.md "Cache-purge needed on every Cloudflare deploy" item) — extra-important for this commit because every prerendered page changed both content and security-header surface.
5. **Search Console.** Register `daylila.com` as a new domain property; submit `https://daylila.com/sitemap.xml`. The 301 from #3 carries most of Google's signal across, but the new property needs to exist for Coverage and Search-Performance data to start accruing on the new domain.
6. **OG image cache.** No operator action — but the cached version of the old "zeemish" PNG persists at every social platform that previously fetched it. Twitter/Facebook/LinkedIn/Slack/iMessage cache OG images per URL for days–weeks; old shares of `zeemish.io/...` URLs continue to render the old card until the platform's cache expires. New shares post-deploy get the fresh "daylila" PNG immediately.

**Deferred to a later phase** (NOT part of this commit):
- **GitHub repo rename** (`zzeeshann/zeemish-v2` → `zzeeshann/daylila`). Would break Publisher's `REPO_NAME = 'zeemish-v2'` constant and the CI deploy paths until both are updated in lockstep. Sequenced separately so the repo rename, the CI workflow update, and the worker `service:` binding rename can all land together.
- **Breathing-tools DNS retirement** (`listens.zeemish.io`, `api.zeemish.io`). Different earlier product entirely; sweeping it up alongside this rebrand would conflate two cleanups. Deferred.

**Why this is in DECISIONS.md and not just CLAUDE.md.** The rebrand is a load-bearing decision that future readers of this codebase need context on — why some bindings are still named `zeemish-*` (internal infrastructure deferred), why the existing-entries section of this same file talks about a domain called `zeemish.io` (truth at the time), why `agents/src/publisher.ts` still pushes to a repo called `zeemish-v2` (deferred lockstep rename). The full reasoning belongs in the append-only log, not in living docs that get rewritten.

## 2026-05-05: InteractiveGenerator parse-retry Layer 3 — JSON-repair revision on parse-fail (option C, holding for Phase 2 root-cause work)

The 2026-05-05 daily piece (observation/expectation theme) ran post-publish and InteractiveGenerator failed BOTH paths across all 3 rounds with `parseAndValidate*: Claude returned non-JSON output`. Dashboard heads showed an identical defect across all 6 attempts (3 quiz + 3 HTML, different drafts each round): the `concept` value was emitted unquoted after a correctly-quoted `"slug"` and `"title"`. Manual admin retrigger succeeded immediately on both paths — confirming the wobble is stochastic, not deterministic, and the model CAN produce valid JSON for this piece.

Investigation surfaced a STRUCTURAL gap distinct from the wobble itself (which is the [observing] 2026-05-03 entry's subject and remains queued for Phase 2 root-cause work): **rounds 2 and 3 silently re-ran the initial prompt with NO signal that the previous output was malformed.** The audit-revise path only fires when JSON parsed but auditor failed; the catch-and-continue catch on parse-fail fell back to the initial-produce branch (the `if (round === 1 || !lastQuiz || !lastAudit)` check landed there because `lastQuiz` was never set). So whenever the wobble repeats across sampling variation, the loop hammered the same prompt 3 times for no reason.

**The fix (Layer 3).** Track parse-fail state separately. New private state `lastParseFailHead: string | null` in both loops at `agents/src/interactive-generator.ts:runQuizLoop` and `:runHtmlLoop`. The catch handler that recognises `parseAndValidate*: Claude returned non-JSON output` pushes the head to `parseFailures` AND populates `lastParseFailHead`. The next round's branch logic checks `lastParseFailHead` FIRST (priority over both `produceQuiz`/`produceHtml` and `reviseQuiz`/`reviseHtml`) and routes through new `repairQuiz` / `repairHtml` private methods. Those methods send `buildJsonRepairPrompt` / `buildHtmlJsonRepairPrompt` (new functions in `agents/src/interactive-generator-prompt.ts`) as the user message — quoting the malformed-output head back to Claude with explicit instructions to re-emit valid JSON. `lastParseFailHead` clears on any successful parse so subsequent rounds resume the normal produce-then-revise cadence (and so audit-feedback revisions take priority over JSON-repair when both could apply across a round boundary).

The repair prompt is small — previous-attempt block (the broken head), a "What to do" block (re-emit as valid JSON, explicit "every string value MUST be enclosed in double quotes" guidance, exact JSON shape, decline option), the same piece block + recent block as the initial prompt. System prompt unchanged (`INTERACTIVE_GENERATOR_PROMPT` / `INTERACTIVE_HTML_GENERATOR_PROMPT`); same `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929` model; same `max_tokens` (3000 quiz, 16000 HTML); same assistant-prefill `{`; same cache_control on the HTML system block (cache prefix matches across produce/revise/repair so cache hits stay clean).

**What this fixes vs. what it doesn't.** Layer 3 fixes the structural retry-blindness: every parse-fail round now gets a different prompt to look at, so a single Claude wobble doesn't burn the whole 3-round budget. Layer 3 does NOT fix the wobble itself — Claude can still occasionally drop quotes around long natural-language `concept` values on any given round; we're just not blindly hammering it. The deeper fixes ([observing] 2026-05-03's "Three forward fixes" — structured-output JSON mode, tighter prefill, JSON-repair preprocessor) all remain queued for after Foundation Fix Phase 2.

**Why ship this now (during Foundation Fix Phase 2 prep).** The user explicitly approved Layer 3 because it doesn't touch Phase 2 territory: no contract content moves, no canonical files, no API-shape changes, no codegen surface. Two new private methods, two new prompt-builder functions, one extra branch + one extra state variable per loop. Pure additive code in two files. The verifier `agents/scripts/verify-parse-retry.mjs` was extended to assert the new branch routing (4 cases: success on round 1 with no repair calls, parse-fail-then-repair-success with broken-head propagation visible to the repair stub, all-rounds-fail with terminal throw, repair-flag-clear-after-parse-success). The deeper fixes can land cleanly post-Phase-2 without first un-doing this layer.

**Why option (a) wasn't picked.** Anthropic tool-calling for forced JSON-schema output (option (a) in this session's investigation) is the deeper fix — eliminates the wobble at the API contract layer rather than working around it. But it touches all 6 Claude call sites in InteractiveGenerator, reshapes the response-content path (tool_use block, not text), and the HTML path's long HTML-string-as-tool-input has unverified character-length implications. More change to ship at once, more places it can break. (c) — JSON-repair revision — is a half-day surgical change that validates the diagnosis cheaply and gathers data on whether the structural retry-blindness was the dominant issue or whether the wobble itself needs API-level enforcement.

**Verification.** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean on the two touched files (the 26 pre-existing `src/server.ts` Durable Object errors stay unchanged). `pnpm verify-parse-retry` 4/4 with extended cases. `pnpm verify-contracts-fresh` ✓ (no codegen surface touched). `pnpm verify-interactive-voice` 10/10 (no rule-body changes). Manual greps confirm: every parse-fail catch in `interactive-generator.ts` now populates `lastParseFailHead`; every loop branch checks it first; every successful-parse path clears it; the new prompt builders are pure functions of their inputs (no module-level state).

**Live observation watch.** Track parse-fail rate across both paths over the next 30 cron pieces. Pre-Layer-3 healthy mid-band per the [observing] 2026-05-03 entry was ≤1 all-3-round failure per 14 cron pieces (=12% across both paths). Post-Layer-3 the band tightens to ≤1 all-3-round failure per 30 cron pieces (=~3%) since each round now has a different prompt to recover from. Above that, escalate to the Phase-2 root-cause work.

**Files touched.** `agents/src/interactive-generator-prompt.ts` (added `buildJsonRepairPrompt` + `buildHtmlJsonRepairPrompt`); `agents/src/interactive-generator.ts` (added `repairQuiz` + `repairHtml` private methods, added `lastParseFailHead` state + new branch in both loops, updated catch handlers); `agents/scripts/verify-parse-retry.mjs` (extended to assert repair-branch routing); `CLAUDE.md` (agent #15 description appended); `docs/AGENTS.md` (Parse-retry hardening section grew from two-layer to three-layer); `docs/FOLLOWUPS.md` (2026-05-03 [observing] entry got an "Update 2026-05-05 — Layer 3 mitigation shipped" addendum).

**What's NOT changed.** No contract content. No codegen output. No site-side files. No API shape. Round budget still 3. `INTERACTIVE_MAX_ROUNDS` unchanged. Audit dimensions unchanged. Validator unchanged. Auditor prompt unchanged. The wobble itself is unchanged — Claude still occasionally drops quotes around long values; Layer 3 just gives the next round a different prompt rather than the same one. Hard rule intact: published pieces are permanent. The 2026-05-05 piece's interactives that were manually retriggered already shipped; this session does not regenerate them.

## 2026-05-10: Categoriser cluster extracted to `content/categoriser-contract.md` (Foundation Fix Task 02 eighth and FINAL extraction session — Phase 1 complete)

Following Phase A (2026-05-03 — codegen plumbing + voice contract + html reference), beats (2026-05-04), interactive (2026-05-05), audit-thresholds (2026-05-06), fact-check (2026-05-07), curator (2026-05-08), and audio (2026-05-09), this session extracted the eighth and final cluster — categoriser. Six rules collapsed across the categoriser system prompt, the resolver, two literal SQL strings (one per worker), and one site-side TS constant into a single `content/categoriser-contract.md` canonical: the 1–3 assignment cap, the 75-confidence ideal-reuse floor, the 60-confidence stretch-reuse floor, the empty-array prohibition + single-retry recovery + last-resort fallback path, the reserved `patterns-yet-to-cluster` slug, and the taxonomy-evolution discipline (durable subjects, ≥10 future pieces, kebab-case slug, AT MOST one new category per run). Behaviour-preserving — no value changes; rule bodies unchanged from the canonical prose previously trapped in `agents/src/categoriser-prompt.ts:38-104` (system prompt) and `:163-169` (retry message), plus the literal SQL strings at `agents/src/director.ts:1697` and `src/pages/api/daily/[date]/made.ts:324`, plus the site-side `FALLBACK_SLUG` at `src/lib/categories.ts:26`.

**The change.** New `content/categoriser-contract.md` (~6 KB). New SOURCES row in `agents/scripts/codegen-contracts.mjs` produces `CATEGORISER_CONTRACT` in `agents/src/shared/generated/contracts.ts` (now 67,224 bytes; nine exports: VOICE_CONTRACT, INTERACTIVE_HTML_REFERENCE, BEAT_CONTRACT, INTERACTIVE_CONTRACT, AUDIT_CONTRACT, FACT_CHECK_CONTRACT, CURATOR_CONTRACT, AUDIO_CONTRACT, CATEGORISER_CONTRACT). New `agents/src/shared/categoriser-thresholds.ts` exports the four named constants (`CATEGORISER_MAX_ASSIGNMENTS = 3`, `CATEGORISER_REUSE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR = 75`, `CATEGORISER_REUSE_CONFIDENCE_STRETCH = 60`, `CATEGORISER_FALLBACK_SLUG = 'patterns-yet-to-cluster'`). New `src/lib/categoriser-thresholds.ts` exports just `FALLBACK_SLUG` for the asymmetric site-side mirror (header docstring naming the contract canonical + the agents-side file as parallel, same shape as fact-check's site-side mirror). Five code-side edits in five files: `categoriser-prompt.ts` rewritten to inject `${CATEGORISER_CONTRACT}` and strip the now-duplicated `# Hard rule` / `# The most important rule` / `# Tiered decision` / `# Assignment shape` sections (response-format JSON spec preserved inline; retry-message preserved inline with `60` / `74` literals substituted for the dropped `${CATEGORISER_REUSE_CONFIDENCE_STRETCH}` / `${CATEGORISER_REUSE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR - 1}` template literals); the four constants moved to the new shared module and re-exported from `categoriser-prompt.ts` for back-compat with external readers; `categoriser.ts:14` swaps the import path for the three constants it consumes from `./categoriser-prompt` to `./shared/categoriser-thresholds`; `director.ts:1697` swaps the literal SQL `c.slug != 'patterns-yet-to-cluster'` for `c.slug != ?` with `CATEGORISER_FALLBACK_SLUG` bound parameter (new import); `categories.ts:26` swaps its inline `FALLBACK_SLUG` literal for an import + re-export from the new site-side mirror (preserves `account.astro:9`'s existing import path); `made.ts:324` swaps the literal SQL `c.slug != 'patterns-yet-to-cluster'` for `c.slug != ?` with bound `FALLBACK_SLUG` (new import). Migration 0027's seed-row literal stays — deliberate non-change.

**Q1–Q9 resolutions.**

1. **Contract scope — wider, including the *why* across all six rules.** Numbers/lists alone would lose the rationale. The contract carries the 1–3 cap with both halves explained ("the lower bound matters as much as the upper" — empty array never valid; "three is an upper bound, not a target" — don't pad), the 75-floor "ideal reuse" semantics, the 60–74 "stretch reuse" tier with the required reasoning sentence semantics ("thematic echo, not primary subject" / "adjacent mechanism, not core") and the "keeps the taxonomy converging rather than fragmenting" rationale, the new-category constraints (subject not topic-of-the-week, ≥10 future pieces, kebab-case slug under 4 words), the at-most-one-new-per-run discipline, the empty-array → single-retry → last-resort-fallback recovery sequence in three numbered steps, and the `patterns-yet-to-cluster` slug's hidden-from-every-reader-facing-surface posture (chip bar, per-category page, made-drawer, account observation). Mirrors the fact-check Q1 / curator Q1 / audio Q1 resolutions exactly.
2. **Cross-worker mirror — asymmetric, fact-check Q2 shape.** Site enforces only the fallback-slug filter at render time (chip bar / 404 / drawer / account); floors and assignment cap are agent-side rules (resolver enforces before the row reaches `piece_categories`). New `agents/src/shared/categoriser-thresholds.ts` exports four constants; new `src/lib/categoriser-thresholds.ts` exports one (`FALLBACK_SLUG`). Same name root on both sides for import-grep symmetry, parallel to `fact-check-thresholds.ts` and `audit-thresholds.ts`. Header docstrings on both sides explain the asymmetry. Stricter than audit Q2 (which had symmetric mirror because site enforced render-time tier mapping); same asymmetry as fact-check Q2 (which had asymmetric mirror because consumers genuinely differ on each side).
3. **Shared module + duplicate-consumer unification.** Two real duplications close in this session: `'patterns-yet-to-cluster'` literal SQL string in `director.ts:1697` (Curator-context filter, agents-side) → swaps to `?` with bound `CATEGORISER_FALLBACK_SLUG`; same literal SQL in `made.ts:324` (drawer "Filed under" filter, site-side) → swaps to `?` with bound `FALLBACK_SLUG`. The site-side `FALLBACK_SLUG` const at `categories.ts:26` collapses to a re-export from the new `src/lib/categoriser-thresholds.ts`, preserving `account.astro:9`'s existing import path through `categories.ts` (no churn). Same precedent as audio Q3 (`CHAR_CAP` / `MAX_BEATS_PER_CHUNK` collapsing duplicate consumers via shared module).
4. **Prompt injection — INJECT `${CATEGORISER_CONTRACT}`.** Beats / interactive / fact-check / curator precedent: rule body in prose belongs in the prompt at runtime via `${...}` injection. The categoriser system prompt has substantial RULE BODY (tiered decision + reuse bias + fallback path + taxonomy-evolution discipline) — exactly what `${...}` injection was designed for. Behaviour-preserving: the prompt's rule prose moves into the contract body and Claude reads the same English text via interpolation. Unlike audit-thresholds and audio (no injection because no Claude call has rule body in prose), categoriser is a Claude call with substantial prose rule body — same posture as curator Q6.
5. **System-vs-user split — preserved with retry-message inline.** `CATEGORISER_PROMPT` is the system prompt (cacheable): persona + scaffolding + `${CATEGORISER_CONTRACT}` + Response-format JSON shape spec + "No prose. No markdown fences." trailer. User message = `buildCategoriserPrompt(piece, existing)` — the piece block + categories block, per-call data, no rule body. Retry follow-up = inline `CATEGORISER_RETRY_MESSAGE` user turn. The `60` and `74` numeric literals in the retry message stay inline (codegen JSON.stringify's the markdown verbatim, so template-literal interpolations in the inline retry string would never reach Claude — same shape as fact-check Q4's `max_uses = 8` retry context). Hand-synced with the contract by the same human. Response-format JSON spec stays inline below the contract injection — TypeScript-typed contract on response shape, not rule body about *what* to assign. Same posture as audit Q5 / fact-check Q5 / curator Q5.
6. **File naming.** `content/categoriser-contract.md` (mirrors voice/beat/interactive/audit/fact-check/curator/audio). `CATEGORISER_CONTRACT` codegen export. `agents/src/shared/categoriser-thresholds.ts` and `src/lib/categoriser-thresholds.ts` — same name on both sides, matching audit-thresholds and fact-check-thresholds precedent. Branch `foundation-fix-02-extraction-categoriser` off the verified audio tip `ad2cfdd`.
7. **No new verifier — existing one keeps its body, gets a header pointer.** `agents/scripts/verify-categoriser-floor.mjs` already covers the resolver shape (empty-array → retry, sub-floor → retry, stretch-reuse kept, boundary at 60 kept, boundary at 59 dropped, malformed JSON falls through). Header comment at `:25-26` updated to name `content/categoriser-contract.md` as canonical rule body and `agents/src/shared/categoriser-thresholds.ts` as canonical values; the inlined `60` and `3` at `:29-30` stay as hand-synced regression copies. Same posture as `verify-fact-checker.mjs` and `verify-interactive-voice.mjs`.
8. **Deliberate non-changes.** Migration 0027's seed-row literal `'patterns-yet-to-cluster'` stays — SQL migrations cannot import TS constants; the migration runs idempotently once per environment via `INSERT OR IGNORE`. The contract's "How agents apply this contract" section names this as deliberate non-change. Voice contract not injected into Categoriser — Categoriser returns JSON, not reader-facing prose, so voice rules don't apply. CATEGORISER_RETRY_MESSAGE prose unchanged (only the dropped template-literal interpolations replaced with their literal values). Resolver / control-flow logic unchanged at `categoriser.ts:447-526` — imports change, behaviour doesn't. The narrative comment at `curator-prompt.ts:71` mentioning the `patterns-yet-to-cluster` slug is documentation context, not a rule use-site, left as-is.
9. **Book + glossary.** `book/09-the-sixteen-roles.md` Categoriser section (section 13) gets a "Where the rule lives" paragraph in the same shape as Voice Auditor's "Where the 85 lives" / Curator's "Where the rule lives" / Audio Producer's "Where the rule lives". `book/99-glossary.md` gains a new alphabetical "Categoriser contract" entry between "Beat contract" and "Claude"; existing "Codegen" entry source-count bumps from "seven extracted clusters" to "eight (and final Phase 1) extracted clusters" with categoriser-contract.md and 2026-05-10 date appended to the inline list.

**Verification.** `pnpm codegen` ✓ (67,224 bytes — nine exports), `pnpm verify-contracts-fresh` ✓, `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean on the touched files (the 26 pre-existing `src/server.ts` Durable Object errors stay unchanged), full `pnpm verify-*` suite green (verify-categoriser-floor 10/0, verify-splice 4/0, verify-normalize 20/0, verify-validator 28/0, verify-dedup 13/0, verify-interactive-voice 10/0, verify-parse-retry 4/0, verify-pair-slug 5/0, verify-fact-checker 9/0). Manual greps confirm zero remaining inline `c.slug != 'patterns-yet-to-cluster'` SQL literals outside the migration 0027 seed row data; zero remaining inline `CATEGORISER_REUSE_CONFIDENCE_FLOOR = ` / `CATEGORISER_REUSE_CONFIDENCE_STRETCH = ` / `CATEGORISER_MAX_ASSIGNMENTS = ` definitions outside the canonical `categoriser-thresholds.ts`; the `patterns-yet-to-cluster` slug appears only at the canonical agents-side const, the canonical site-side const, the contract markdown, the migration 0027 seed-row data, and the `curator-prompt.ts:71` narrative comment (all expected).

**Forward observation.** Same risk profile as fact-check / interactive / beats / curator content shifts — the Categoriser system prompt now reads materially differently than before (rule restatements gone, contract injection in their place). Watch the next 3–5 cron-generated pieces for: Categoriser failure rate (baseline zero in last 30 days; any non-zero is a regression), retry-firing rate (baseline <5% of runs), fallback-firing rate (baseline very rare; last hit 2026-04-28 golden-orb), novel-category rate (baseline 10–20%). If signals shift materially, revert via `git revert` on the extraction commit (rolls back atomically — codegen regenerates `contracts.ts` from the rolled-back canonical files; migration 0027 untouched).

**Prompt-cache impact.** System prompt grows by contract length (~6 KB of categoriser rule body now lives there); user message and retry message unchanged in shape (the retry's `60` / `74` literals replaced template-literal interpolations one-for-one). Net positive cacheable-fraction — the system prompt is cacheable across calls, user message is per-call.

**What's NOT changed.** No rule values modified. The 1–3 cap, 75 floor, 60 stretch floor, `patterns-yet-to-cluster` slug, retry-then-fallback recovery sequence, taxonomy-evolution discipline — all preserved verbatim. The InteractiveGenerator JSON-quote-drop wobble (`[observing] 2026-05-03`) remains deferred until Foundation Fix completes. The [observing] 2026-05-01 Curator + Scanner diversity verification window is **explicitly not escalated** in this session — it's an observation task, this is a rule-extraction task — but its `unblock` date 2026-05-08 has already passed (curator session) and any unblock-tweak that requires editing rule body now lands cleanly in `content/curator-contract.md` and (for the 75-confidence novel-propose floor referenced in failure-mode A) in `content/categoriser-contract.md`. The [wontfix] 2026-05-01 "don't pre-create empty categories" entry is honoured — no taxonomy seeding. Hard rule intact: published pieces are permanent. Frontmatter `pieceCategories` splice (the explicit carve-out) is unchanged.

**Phase 1 of Foundation Fix closes here.** All eight rule clusters extracted: voice 2026-05-03, beats 2026-05-04, interactive 2026-05-05, audit-thresholds 2026-05-06, fact-check 2026-05-07, curator 2026-05-08, audio 2026-05-09, categoriser 2026-05-10. Every inline rule-body restatement has been pulled into a canonical markdown contract; the agents bundle reaches them via codegen at build time; the CI gate (`pnpm verify-contracts-fresh`) blocks deploy on any drift between canonical files and the bundled module. The next phases of the Foundation Fix programme (`docs/foundation-fix/03-CURATOR-FIX.md`, `04-LEARNER-LOOP.md`, and onward) begin next.

**Branch:** `foundation-fix-02-extraction-categoriser` off `ad2cfdd` (the audio branch tip; the audio PR hadn't merged to main at session start, so this branches off the audio work directly to keep iterative work building cleanly. Eventual merge order is beats PR → main → quiz PR rebased on top → main → audit-thresholds PR rebased on top → main → fact-check PR rebased on top → main → curator PR rebased on top → main → audio PR rebased on top → main → categoriser PR rebased on top).

## 2026-05-09: Audio cluster extracted to `content/audio-contract.md` (Foundation Fix Task 02 seventh extraction session)

Following Phase A (2026-05-03 — codegen plumbing + voice contract + html reference), beats (2026-05-04), interactive (2026-05-05), audit-thresholds (2026-05-06), fact-check (2026-05-07), and curator (2026-05-08), this session extracted the seventh cluster — audio. Six rule constants collapsed across nine surfaces into a single `content/audio-contract.md` canonical: ElevenLabs voice (`j9jfwdrw7BRfcR43Qohk` Frederick Surrey), model (`eleven_multilingual_v2`), output format (`mp3_44100_96`), character cap (`20_000` per piece), retry count (3 attempts), and per-call beats budget (default 2). Behaviour-preserving — no value changes; rule values + canonical phrasings unchanged from `agents/src/audio-producer.ts:58-67,122,309`, `agents/src/audio-auditor.ts:40,145,148` (defense-in-depth duplicate of CHAR_CAP), and `agents/src/director.ts:1296` (call-site duplicate of MAX_BEATS_PER_CHUNK).

**The change.** New `content/audio-contract.md` (~75 lines). New SOURCES row in `agents/scripts/codegen-contracts.mjs` produces `AUDIO_CONTRACT` in `agents/src/shared/generated/contracts.ts` (now 56,520 bytes; eight exports: VOICE_CONTRACT, INTERACTIVE_HTML_REFERENCE, BEAT_CONTRACT, INTERACTIVE_CONTRACT, AUDIT_CONTRACT, FACT_CHECK_CONTRACT, CURATOR_CONTRACT, AUDIO_CONTRACT). New `agents/src/shared/audio-thresholds.ts` exports six AUDIO_-prefixed constants — `AUDIO_VOICE_ID`, `AUDIO_MODEL_ID`, `AUDIO_OUTPUT_FORMAT`, `AUDIO_CHAR_CAP`, `AUDIO_MAX_RETRIES`, `AUDIO_BEATS_PER_CHUNK`. Agents-only — no site-side mirror (the site worker does not enforce audio rules; the display-side label lookups in `made-drawer.ts:296,303` are render-time reads against persisted DB columns, with graceful fallback if values change). Three code-side edits in three files: `audio-producer.ts` swaps four local `const`s + the `AudioBudgetExceededError` default cap + the `maxBeats` parameter default + the retry-loop `attempt <= 3` literal for imported names (six use-site replacements); `audio-auditor.ts` swaps the local `CHAR_CAP = 20_000` defense-in-depth duplicate for an `AUDIO_CHAR_CAP` import (two use-site replacements at the cap-check + the user-facing issue text); `director.ts:1296` swaps the local `MAX_BEATS_PER_CHUNK = 2` for an aliased import `AUDIO_BEATS_PER_CHUNK as MAX_BEATS_PER_CHUNK` (preserves in-file readability — same posture as `MAX_REVISIONS = MAX_AUDIT_ROUNDS` from audit-thresholds Q3).

**Q1–Q9 resolutions.**

1. **Contract scope — wider, including the *why* across all six rules.** Numbers/lists alone would lose the rationale. The contract carries the My Voices stability rationale (the voice ID survives shared-library removals because added to operator's library), the multilingual model choice (handles non-English proper nouns without prosody collapse), the 96 kbps trade-off (indistinguishable from 128 for single-voice narration, ~25% smaller R2 footprint + egress, audit's expected-bytes-per-character bound is calibrated against this bitrate), the 20k cap sizing (12-beat newspaper-style piece × ~200 words/beat + headroom), the retry-policy shape (4xx no-retry / 5xx + network do retry, 90s per-attempt AbortSignal.timeout, 1s/2s exponential backoff), and the per-call beats budget rationale (Cloudflare DO RPC ~30s wall-clock ceiling — empirically proven on 2026-04-19). Mirrors the audit Q1 / fact-check Q1 / curator Q1 resolutions exactly.
2. **Cross-worker mirror — agents-only, asymmetric posture.** Audio is agents-only today; the site worker does not enforce audio rules at render time. The made-drawer.ts:296 literal-match `a.model === 'eleven_multilingual_v2'` is a friendly-label lookup against the persisted `daily_piece_audio.model` column (graceful `?? 'ElevenLabs'` fallback if values change); made-drawer.ts:303 hardcodes `Frederick Surrey` as a display name (producer doesn't know the human name). Both are display-side, NOT rule mirrors — same posture as the `'contested'` legacy alias dispositioned in fact-check Q3. New `agents/src/shared/audio-thresholds.ts` lives only on the agents side. Same shape as curator-thresholds (DECISIONS 2026-05-08 Q2 — agents-only, no site-side mirror).
3. **Shared module + duplicate-consumer unification.** Two real duplications close in this session: `CHAR_CAP = 20_000` in producer (rule canonical) + auditor (defense-in-depth) → both import `AUDIO_CHAR_CAP`; `MAX_BEATS_PER_CHUNK = 2` in producer (default param) + Director (call-site safety belt) → both import `AUDIO_BEATS_PER_CHUNK`. Same precedent as audit-thresholds Q3 (`MAX_AUDIT_ROUNDS` unifying Director's `MAX_REVISIONS` and InteractiveGenerator's `INTERACTIVE_MAX_ROUNDS`). Director keeps the local `MAX_BEATS_PER_CHUNK` name as an `as`-aliased import for in-file readability — same shape as `MAX_REVISIONS = MAX_AUDIT_ROUNDS` in audit-thresholds.
4. **Naming — AUDIO_ prefix on all six.** `AUDIO_VOICE_ID` / `AUDIO_MODEL_ID` / `AUDIO_OUTPUT_FORMAT` / `AUDIO_CHAR_CAP` / `AUDIO_MAX_RETRIES` / `AUDIO_BEATS_PER_CHUNK`. Consistent prefix avoids conceptual collision at use-sites (the audit-thresholds module exports `VOICE_PASS_THRESHOLD` — un-prefixed `VOICE_ID` would read confusingly alongside it in any file that imports both). Also future-proofs against any other `MODEL_ID` constant ever landing in the same import scope.
5. **Prompt injection — NONE today.** Audio Producer makes zero Claude calls (TTS-only via ElevenLabs HTTP); Audio Auditor makes zero Claude calls (R2 HEAD checks only). Codegen still produces `AUDIO_CONTRACT` for canonical-source consistency and so a future post-publish audio narrator (a podcast intro generator, an audio-quality summariser) can opt-in via `${...}` injection without redesign. The verifier-fresh check still catches drift on the canonical markdown. Same Q6 disposition as audit-thresholds (DECISIONS 2026-05-06 Q6).
6. **What stays inline (deliberate non-changes).** The 90-second `AbortSignal.timeout(90_000)` at audio-producer.ts:319 — single use-site, narrated in contract under retry policy, comment block at lines 282–289 documents the sizing. Hoisting to a 7th constant would be premature abstraction. The five-key `voice_settings` object (`stability: 0.6`, `similarity_boost: 0.75`, `style: 0.3`, `use_speaker_boost: true`, `speed: 0.95`) at audio-producer.ts:299–303 — TTS provider tuning, single HTTP-call surface, narrated in contract for completeness, stays inline. The auditor's `EXPECTED_BYTES_PER_CHAR = 960` / `MIN_SIZE_RATIO = 0.3` / `MAX_SIZE_RATIO = 3.0` / `< 50 chars` warning — auditor sanity bounds, separate cluster (audio-audit thresholds), not in scope.
7. **No new verifier; no other prompt injections.** No new `verify-audio.mjs` — Producer behaviour is HTTP/R2/D1 implementation, not parser/threshold-loop in TS code. Same posture as audit-thresholds (no new verifier added) and curator (no new verifier added). The existing `verify-normalize.mjs` covers the TTS text normaliser (separate cluster — Roman-numeral conversion + Zeemish prosody alias). Director, Drafter, auditors don't read audio rules — documented as deliberate non-change per fact-check Q8.
8. **Names.** `content/audio-contract.md` (mirrors voice/beat/interactive/audit/fact-check/curator). `AUDIO_CONTRACT` codegen export. `agents/src/shared/audio-thresholds.ts` (file name describes the constants, parallel to `audit-thresholds.ts` / `fact-check-thresholds.ts` / `curator-thresholds.ts`). Branch `foundation-fix-02-extraction-audio` off the verified curator tip `ed3590a`.
9. **Book + glossary.** `book/13-audio-pipeline.md` (currently outline-status) gets a "Where the rule lives" paragraph in the same shape as Voice Auditor's "Where the 85 lives" / Curator's "Where the rule lives" / Fact Checker's "Where the rule lives". `book/09-the-sixteen-roles.md` Audio Producer entry (section 10) gains a parallel "Where the rule lives" pointer; Audio Auditor entry (section 11) gains a one-sentence pointer noting the defense-in-depth import. `book/99-glossary.md` gains a new alphabetical "Audio contract" entry between "Audit (in Zeemish)" and "Beat"; existing "Codegen" entry source-count bumps from "six extracted clusters" to "seven extracted clusters" with audio-contract.md and 2026-05-09 date appended to the inline list.

**Verification.** `pnpm codegen` ✓ (56,520 bytes — eight exports), `pnpm verify-contracts-fresh` ✓, `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean on the touched files (the 26 pre-existing `src/server.ts` Durable Object errors stay unchanged), full `pnpm verify-*` suite green (verify-splice, verify-normalize, verify-validator, verify-dedup, verify-categoriser-floor, verify-interactive-voice, verify-parse-retry, verify-pair-slug, verify-fact-checker — all green), manual greps confirm zero remaining inline literal `j9jfwdrw7BRfcR43Qohk` / `eleven_multilingual_v2` / `mp3_44100_96` / `20_000` / `MAX_BEATS_PER_CHUNK = 2` outside the canonical sources (the surviving `eleven_multilingual_v2` matches are: audio-producer.ts:284 narrative comment about 3000-char latency, audio-thresholds.ts module file, made-drawer.ts:296 site-side display lookup, auto-generated contracts.ts — all expected).

**Forward observation.** Lower risk than prompt-content shifts in prior sessions — this extraction touches code-side constants only (numeric value-replacements). The TTS payload to ElevenLabs is byte-identical (the URL template, model_id, and voice_settings produce the same JSON), and the producer/auditor behaviour is unchanged. No Claude-side prompt content shifted. If any signal shifts materially on the next 3–5 cron pieces (audio failure rate, R2 size deviations, retry-count distribution), revert via `git revert` on the extraction commit (rolls back atomically — codegen regenerates `contracts.ts` from the rolled-back canonical files).

The InteractiveGenerator JSON-quote-drop wobble (`[observing] 2026-05-03`) remains deferred until Foundation Fix completes. The `[observing] 2026-05-01 Curator + Scanner diversity verification window` is observation, not extraction — not escalated this session. The auditor's size-sanity bounds (`EXPECTED_BYTES_PER_CHAR`, `MIN_SIZE_RATIO`, `MAX_SIZE_RATIO`, very-short-text < 50 chars warning) are a separate cluster (audio-audit thresholds) and not in scope. The site-side display-label literals at `made-drawer.ts:296,303` are kept inline as documented self-mirrors per fact-check Q3 precedent. The narrative comment at `audio-producer.ts:284` mentioning `eleven_multilingual_v2` latency context is prose, not duplication. Hard rule intact: published pieces are permanent. ## 2026-05-08: Curator cluster extracted to `content/curator-contract.md` (Foundation Fix Task 02 sixth extraction session)

Following Phase A (2026-05-03 — codegen plumbing + voice contract + html reference), the beats cluster (2026-05-04), the interactive cluster (2026-05-05), the audit-thresholds cluster (2026-05-06), and the fact-check cluster (2026-05-07), this session extracted the sixth cluster — curator. Five rules collapsed across the curator system prompt and user-message builder into a single `content/curator-contract.md` canonical: the five selection criteria (TEACHABILITY / UNIVERSALITY / FRESHNESS / DEPTH POTENTIAL / NO TRIBAL FRAMING), the 10-domain breadth taxonomy embedded under TEACHABILITY, the recent-category concentration soft skip ("3+ in 30 days"), the SAME-EVENT and SAME-CONCEPT hard skips with three worked examples, and the skip output shape (`{skip: true, reason}` with the "reason must NOT be a category dismissal" instruction). Behaviour-preserving — no value changes; rule bodies unchanged from `agents/src/curator-prompt.ts:24-98` (system prompt rule body) and `agents/src/curator-prompt.ts:128-142` (user-message rule body).

**The change.** New `content/curator-contract.md` (~85 lines). New SOURCES row in `agents/scripts/codegen-contracts.mjs` produces `CURATOR_CONTRACT` in `agents/src/shared/generated/contracts.ts` (now 48,464 bytes; seven exports: VOICE_CONTRACT, INTERACTIVE_HTML_REFERENCE, BEAT_CONTRACT, INTERACTIVE_CONTRACT, AUDIT_CONTRACT, FACT_CHECK_CONTRACT, CURATOR_CONTRACT). New `agents/src/shared/curator-thresholds.ts` exports `CURATOR_RECENT_WINDOW_DAYS = 30` — agents-only (no site-side mirror; the site worker does not read curator rules at render time, asymmetric posture vs. audit-thresholds and fact-check-thresholds which both have site-side consumers). Three code-side edits in two files: `curator-prompt.ts` rewritten to inject `${CURATOR_CONTRACT}` between the inline Zeemish Protocol opener and the response-format JSON spec; `buildCuratorPrompt` user-message rule prose collapsed to a 2-3 line Tier-2 audit-context paraphrase under each data block (beats Q6 precedent — "swap the bytes, keep the structure"); `director.ts:207` `getRecentDailyPieces(30)` → `getRecentDailyPieces(CURATOR_RECENT_WINDOW_DAYS)` and `director.ts:245` `getRecentCategoryCounts(30)` → `getRecentCategoryCounts(CURATOR_RECENT_WINDOW_DAYS)` with new import.

**Q1–Q9 resolutions.**

1. **Contract scope — wider, including the breadth taxonomy + worked pairings + worked examples.** Numbers/lists alone would lose the *why*. The contract carries the five-criteria rubric in priority order, the 10-domain breadth taxonomy embedded as a sub-section under TEACHABILITY (preserving the lift-from-buildCuratorPrompt structure), the worked pairings showing how news shapes map into domains (10 examples), the Default: PICK framing (with the no-piece-day reasoning), the recent-category soft skip rule body with the safety-valve override, the two MUST-skip failure modes with three worked examples carried verbatim from buildCuratorPrompt:139-142, and the skip output shape with the "reason must NOT be a category dismissal" instruction. Mirrors the fact-check Q1 resolution exactly.
2. **Cross-worker mirror — agents-only, asymmetric posture.** Curator is agents-only today; the site worker does not read curator rules. Stricter than fact-check Q2 (which had asymmetric mirror because made-drawer needed the phrase list) — for curator there is no site-side consumer at all. New `agents/src/shared/curator-thresholds.ts` lives only on the agents side. File-header comment names the contract canonical and explicitly documents the no-mirror posture.
3. **Zeemish Protocol opener — kept inline in `CURATOR_PROMPT`.** Voice contract is the Protocol's canonical home (`content/voice-contract.md` opens with the same three-sentence form). Curator never injects voice contract today, and the Protocol-as-lens posture for Curator is documented in DECISIONS 2026-04-25 (the reframe). Adding the Protocol to the curator contract would create duplication with voice-contract.md. Inline preservation in the system prompt (above the contract injection) is the clean call — the contract opens with the curator's job framing one level below the Protocol.
4. **30-day window — extracted to a TS constant.** Two consumer call-sites in director.ts (`getRecentDailyPieces(30)` at :207 and `getRecentCategoryCounts(30)` at :245) + the contract names "30 days" in plain English at three places (the recent-category soft skip section, the SAME-EVENT/SAME-CONCEPT data window framing, the "How agents apply this contract" section). Naming the literal where the contract names it removes the only drift vector. Same precedent as `WEB_SEARCH_MAX_USES = 8` extraction in fact-check Q4. The "3+" threshold within the soft-skip rule stays as inline prose in the contract — no programmatic consumer; only Claude reads "3+".
5. **System-vs-user split — preserved with Tier-2 audit-context paraphrase.** `CURATOR_PROMPT` is the system prompt (cacheable); `buildCuratorPrompt` is the user message (per-call, contains today's data). System prompt: opener + Protocol + `${CURATOR_CONTRACT}` + response-format JSON spec. User message: data blocks (today's candidates, recent pieces, recent category counts) + a 2-3 line audit-context paraphrase under each data block naming the rule by name with no rule body (beats Q6 precedent — "swap the bytes, keep the structure") + the closing nudge `Pick the most teachable story… Two pieces about the same news event or teaching the same concept on the same day is a failure state.` (task framing, not rule body — same posture as audit Q5 / fact-check Q5) + the verbatim-UUID `selectedCandidateId` reminder (response-shape constraint, stays inline).
6. **Prompt injection — INJECT `${CURATOR_CONTRACT}`.** Beats / interactive / fact-check precedent: rule body in prose belongs in the prompt at runtime via `${...}` injection. Audit-thresholds didn't inject because its values flowed through named TS constants only. The curator system prompt has substantial RULE BODY in prose (selection criteria + breadth taxonomy + Default: PICK + skip output shape) — exactly what `${...}` injection was designed for. Behaviour-preserving: the prompt's rule prose moves into the contract body and Claude reads the same English text via interpolation.
7. **No new verifier; no other prompt injections.** No new `verify-curator.mjs` — Curator's behaviour shape is rule prose Claude reads, not a parser/threshold-loop in TS code. Same posture as audit-thresholds (no new verifier added). The existing `verify-dedup.mjs` covers the hard pre-Curator filter at `agents/src/shared/dedup-headlines.ts` — different cluster (Scanner-side filter, defense-in-depth before Curator sees the candidate list). Director makes zero LLM calls (no prompt to inject); Categoriser / Scanner / Drafter / auditors don't read curator rules — documented as deliberate non-change per fact-check Q8.
8. **Names.** `content/curator-contract.md` (mirrors voice/beat/interactive/audit/fact-check). `CURATOR_CONTRACT` codegen export. `agents/src/shared/curator-thresholds.ts` (file name describes the constants, parallel to `audit-thresholds.ts` and `fact-check-thresholds.ts`). `CURATOR_RECENT_WINDOW_DAYS` constant name describes the window. Branch `foundation-fix-02-extraction-curator` off the verified fact-check tip `62ab5bd` (precedent verifies the SHA at session start; this one matched the brief).
9. **Book + glossary.** `book/09-the-sixteen-roles.md` Curator section (paras 29-41) gets a "Where the rule lives" pointer at the end of section 3 in the Voice-Auditor "Where the 85 lives" shape. Chapter narrative on the 10-domain taxonomy + 3+ / 30-day soft-preference rule stays intact (chapter narrative serving the book's reading flow, not enforced rule body — same posture as fact-check leaving `book/05-ai-models.md` alone). `book/99-glossary.md` gains a new alphabetical "Curator contract" entry after "Curator"; existing "Codegen" entry source-count bumps from "five extracted clusters" to "six extracted clusters" with the curator-contract.md and 2026-05-08 date appended to the inline list.

**Verification.** `pnpm codegen` ✓ (48,464 bytes — seven exports), `pnpm verify-contracts-fresh` ✓, `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean on the touched files (the 26 pre-existing `src/server.ts` Durable Object errors stay unchanged), full `pnpm verify-*` suite green (verify-splice, verify-normalize, verify-validator, verify-dedup, verify-categoriser-floor, verify-interactive-voice, verify-parse-retry, verify-pair-slug, verify-fact-checker — all green), manual greps confirm zero remaining inline 5-criteria / 10-domain-taxonomy / SAME-EVENT-SAME-CONCEPT rule-body restatements outside `content/curator-contract.md` (the surviving matches are: scanner.ts:26 + dedup-headlines.ts:9 + director.ts:1655 narrative pointer comments — Q6 future-session sweep; interactive-auditor-prompt.ts:82 uses "SAME UNDERLYING CONCEPT" in a different cluster's audit rationale; curator-prompt.ts:67-68 + :84 are the new audit-context paraphrase; auto-generated contracts.ts is expected).

**Forward observation.** Same risk profile as fact-check / interactive / beats content shifts — the Curator system prompt now reads materially differently than before (rule restatements gone, contract injection in their place). Watch the next 3-5 cron-generated pieces for: skip rate (should stay near zero per Default: PICK), novel-category landings (per the [observing] 2026-05-01 verification window — `unblock` date is 2026-05-08, today's session date), twin-piece incidents (per the [observing] 2026-04-27 architectural-fix recurrence watch), category breadth, and JSON parse stability at `curator.ts:49` (the InteractiveGenerator JSON-quote-drop wobble at `[observing] 2026-05-03` is in a different cluster but the prompt-content shift here could surface a similar Claude-side wobble in Curator's nested `beats[]` JSON; explicit forward-observation signal). If signals shift materially, revert via `git revert` on the extraction commit (rolls back atomically — codegen regenerates `contracts.ts` from the rolled-back canonical files).

**Prompt-cache impact.** System prompt grows by contract length (~5 KB of curator rule body now lives there); user message shrinks (rule prose collapsed to a thin audit-context paraphrase). Net positive cacheable-fraction — the system prompt is cacheable across calls, user message is per-call. Same shape as fact-check Q5.

**What's NOT changed.** No rule values modified. The "30-day window", the "3+ pieces" threshold, the five selection criteria, the 10-domain breadth taxonomy, the SAME-EVENT/SAME-CONCEPT hard-skip language, the worked examples — all preserved verbatim. The InteractiveGenerator JSON-quote-drop wobble (`[observing] 2026-05-03`) remains deferred until Foundation Fix completes. The [observing] 2026-05-01 Curator + Scanner diversity verification window is **explicitly not escalated** in this session — it's an observation task, this is a rule-extraction task — but its `unblock` date 2026-05-08 is today, and any unblock-tweak that requires editing rule body (e.g., tighten threshold from 3+ to 4+, list the 5 thinnest categories explicitly) now lands cleanly in `content/curator-contract.md` instead of buried in prompt prose. The [observing] 2026-04-27 architectural-fix Curator dedup filter watch is also live; the hard pre-Curator filter at `agents/src/shared/dedup-headlines.ts` is a separate cluster (Scanner-side, mirrored by `verify-dedup.mjs`) and untouched. The teachability comment-pointers at `scanner.ts:26`, `dedup-headlines.ts:9-13`, `learner.ts:345`, and `director.ts:1655` (narrative context, not rule restatements) are left as a future-session documentation sweep. The `daily_candidates.teachability_score` column setter at `director.ts:274` (`= 100`) is a use-site of the picked-candidate rule, not duplication. Hard rule intact: published pieces are permanent. Subsequent Foundation Fix Task 02 sessions: audio, categoriser.

**Branch:** `foundation-fix-02-extraction-curator` off `62ab5bd` (the fact-check branch tip; the fact-check PR hadn't merged to main at session start, so this branches off the fact-check work directly to keep iterative work building cleanly. Eventual merge order is beats PR → main → quiz PR rebased on top → main → audit-thresholds PR rebased on top → main → fact-check PR rebased on top → main → curator PR rebased on top).

## 2026-05-07: Fact-check cluster extracted to `content/fact-check-contract.md` (Foundation Fix Task 02 fifth extraction session)

Following Phase A (2026-05-03 — codegen plumbing + voice contract + html reference), the beats cluster (2026-05-04), the interactive cluster (2026-05-05), and the audit-thresholds cluster (2026-05-06), this session extracted the fifth cluster — fact-check. Four rules collapsed across multiple surfaces into a single `content/fact-check-contract.md` canonical: the verdict taxonomy (closed three-value set + asymmetry rule + pass condition), the search-first rule for current-event claims, the cutoff-confession phrase blacklist (across two surfaces with different roles — writer-context prose and matcher-context substrings), and the `max_uses = 8` web-search budget. Behaviour-preserving — no value changes; rule bodies unchanged from the canonical prose previously trapped in `agents/src/fact-checker-prompt.ts:23-42` and the inline phrase array at `src/interactive/made-drawer.ts:531-537`.

**The change.** New `content/fact-check-contract.md` (~85 lines). New SOURCES row in `agents/scripts/codegen-contracts.mjs` produces `FACT_CHECK_CONTRACT` in `agents/src/shared/generated/contracts.ts` (now 36,350 bytes; six exports: VOICE_CONTRACT, INTERACTIVE_HTML_REFERENCE, BEAT_CONTRACT, INTERACTIVE_CONTRACT, AUDIT_CONTRACT, FACT_CHECK_CONTRACT). New `agents/src/shared/fact-check-thresholds.ts` exports the agents-side TS constants (`WEB_SEARCH_MAX_USES = 8` + `CUTOFF_CONFESSION_PHRASES` array carried for surface completeness — agents has no programmatic consumer of the array today, per `TIER_SOLID_FLOOR` precedent). New `src/lib/fact-check-thresholds.ts` mirrors the same array on the site-worker side and adds `CUTOFF_CONFESSION_REPLACEMENT = 'Could not verify against current sources.'` for the drawer's filter (header comment naming the contract canonical + the agents-side file as parallel, same shape as cadence.ts ↔ admin-settings.ts). Three code-side edits in three files: `fact-checker-prompt.ts` rewritten to inject `${FACT_CHECK_CONTRACT}` and strip the now-duplicated CONTEXT/THE WEB SEARCH TOOL/VERDICTS/RULES sections (today's-date pointer + opener + OUTPUT JSON spec preserved); `fact-checker.ts:86` swaps the literal `8` for `WEB_SEARCH_MAX_USES` (with new import); `made-drawer.ts:531-545` swaps the inline `CUTOFF_CONFESSION_PHRASES` array + inline replacement string for imports from the new site-side mirror (header docstring at `:510-530` keeps the historical Venter narrative, gains a one-line contract+constants pointer).

**Q1–Q9 resolutions.**

1. **Contract scope — wider, including the search-grounded architecture rationale.** Numbers/lists alone would lose the *why*. The contract carries the architectural commitment of search-first ("the rule is what lets the verdict taxonomy mean what it says"), the asymmetry rationale (`unverified` is never `incorrect` because absence of evidence is a different statement than contradicting evidence), the cutoff-confession ban with both the longer illustrative phrasings (writer context, what the prompt teaches Claude) AND the canonical 5-substring filter list (matcher context, what the drawer's filter checks) + the `'training data'` dropped rationale per DECISIONS:497, and the FOLLOWUPS escalation note for `max_uses`. Mirrors the audit Q1 resolution exactly.
2. **Cross-worker mirror — parallel TS constants, both files named `fact-check-thresholds.ts`.** Site side carries `CUTOFF_CONFESSION_PHRASES` + `CUTOFF_CONFESSION_REPLACEMENT` (consumed by `made-drawer.ts:541-545` filter); agents side carries `CUTOFF_CONFESSION_PHRASES` (for surface completeness, per `TIER_SOLID_FLOOR` precedent — exported on the agents side though only the site uses it) + `WEB_SEARCH_MAX_USES` (consumed by `fact-checker.ts:86`). Asymmetric exports because consumers genuinely differ on each side: agents calls Anthropic's API (needs the budget); site renders the drawer's defense filter (needs the array + replacement). Symmetric file names match the audit-thresholds precedent exactly; header docstrings on both sides explain what's inside.
3. **Verdict taxonomy — split, same shape as audit Q4 (`qualityFlag`).** Contract body NAMES the three-value taxonomy in plain English (closed set + asymmetry rule + pass condition). The TS literal `'verified' | 'unverified' | 'incorrect'` self-mirrors across six sites — left intentionally unchanged with the literal-as-rule-application posture: `fact-checker.ts:55` (FactClaim type union), `fact-checker.ts:188` (parser status validation), `director.ts:463` (claimReviews splice filter), `integrator.ts:50` (fact-issue feedback filter), `made-drawer.ts:594-597` (status-to-CSS class mapping with `'contested'` legacy alias preserved), `director.ts:1770` (defensive `?? 'unverified'` fallback). The `'contested'` alias is documented in the contract's "How agents apply this contract" section as a render-side back-compat shim for legacy data — NOT added to the canonical taxonomy.
4. **Prompt injection — INJECT `${FACT_CHECK_CONTRACT}`.** Beats and interactive precedent: rule body in prose belongs in the prompt at runtime via `${...}` injection. Audit-thresholds didn't inject because its values flowed through named TS constants only (Director isn't a prompt; auditor prompts only had numeric values in JSON-spec lines). The fact-checker prompt has substantial RULE BODY in prose (CONTEXT/THE WEB SEARCH TOOL/VERDICTS/RULES) — exactly what `${...}` injection was designed for. Behaviour-preserving: the prompt's rule restatements move into the contract body and Claude reads the same English text via interpolation.
5. **System-vs-user split — preserved.** `FACT_CHECKER_PROMPT` is system-only; the user message stays `Today is ${today}. Fact-check this lesson:\n\n${mdx}`. Rule body moves into the contract via system-prompt injection; today's-date pointer + opener line + OUTPUT JSON spec stay inline (response-shape spec is not rule body — same posture as beats Q5 / audit Q5).
6. **File naming — symmetric.** `content/fact-check-contract.md` (mirrors voice/beat/interactive/audit). `FACT_CHECK_CONTRACT` codegen export. `agents/src/shared/fact-check-thresholds.ts` and `src/lib/fact-check-thresholds.ts` — same name on both sides, matching audit-thresholds precedent exactly. The file name describes the topic/cluster; header docstrings explain the per-side contents.
7. **Book + glossary.** `book/09-the-sixteen-roles.md` Fact Checker entry gets a "Where the rule lives" paragraph in the Voice Auditor's "Where the 85 lives" shape. `book/99-glossary.md` gains an alphabetical "Fact-check contract" entry between "Embedder" and "Hugging Face"; existing "Codegen" entry source-count bumps from "five" to "six" and the inline list extends. Existing "Knowledge cutoff" glossary entry is preserved as-is — it's a general definition for readers, not a duplication of the rule.
8. **INTEGRATOR contract injection — NO.** Integrator's prompt (`integrator-prompt.ts`) currently injects `${VOICE_CONTRACT}` and `${BEAT_CONTRACT}` so it can revise voice and structure issues with full context. It already filters fact claims by `c.status !== 'verified'` at integrator.ts:50 to surface fact issues to the next revision round — but Integrator does not re-verdict, it revises prose. Adding `${FACT_CHECK_CONTRACT}` would be a behaviour change (extra prompt tokens, potential subtle Claude shift) for no role-change benefit. Documented here as a deliberate non-change so a future reader doesn't think it was missed.
9. **Verifier-script header.** `agents/scripts/verify-fact-checker.mjs:22-25` gains one paragraph naming the contract canonical and clarifying that the verifier tests parser shape, not rule body. No body change — the 9 test cases continue to assert the three-value status union, the search flag semantics, and the flat-sources URL harvest. Same precedent as `verify-interactive-voice.mjs` getting only a header pointer in the 2026-05-05 session.

**Verification.** `pnpm codegen` ✓ (36,350 bytes), `pnpm verify-contracts-fresh` ✓, `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean on the touched files (the 26 pre-existing `src/server.ts` Durable Object errors stay unchanged), full `pnpm verify-*` suite to be run before commit, manual greps to confirm zero remaining literal `8` for `max_uses` outside the canonical sources, zero remaining inline cutoff-confession phrase arrays outside the new TS modules + contract, zero remaining VERDICTS/RULES inline restatements after extraction.

**Forward observation.** Same risk profile as Phase A / beats / interactive content shifts — the Fact Checker system prompt now reads materially differently than before (rule restatements gone, contract injection in their place). Watch the next 3–5 cron-generated pieces for fact-check verdict shifts (the `searchUsed` / `searchAvailable` flags in observer events; any new failure modes from the changed prompt shape; any cutoff-confession leakage). If signals shift materially, revert via `git revert` on the extraction commit (rolls back atomically — codegen regenerates `contracts.ts` from the rolled-back canonical files).

**What's NOT changed.** No rule values modified. The `max_uses = 8` value is preserved (the FOLLOWUPS escalation note is operator guidance for a future cost decision, not part of this extraction). The diagnostic patch at `interactive-generator.ts:606` (different cluster) and the InteractiveGenerator JSON-quote-drop wobble (`[observing] 2026-05-03`) remain deferred. The Lebanon-shape harvest-from-search-hits parser logic at `fact-checker.ts:141-156` is parser implementation, not rule body — untouched. The drawer's `'contested'` legacy alias at `made-drawer.ts:596` is preserved as a back-compat shim (not added to the canonical taxonomy). The historical narratives in DECISIONS 2026-04-30, FOLLOWUPS 2026-04-30, and `book/05-ai-models.md` (which uses "knowledge cutoff" in a different educational context) are documentation context — not rule body, untouched. Integrator's prompt continues to inject only voice + beat contracts; deliberately not given `${FACT_CHECK_CONTRACT}` (Q8). Subsequent Task 02 sessions: curator, audio, categoriser.

## 2026-05-06: Audit-thresholds cluster extracted to `content/audit-contract.md` (Foundation Fix Task 02 fourth extraction session)

Following Phase A (2026-05-03 — codegen plumbing + voice contract + html reference), the beats cluster (2026-05-04), and the interactive cluster (2026-05-05), this session extracted the fourth cluster — audit-thresholds. Four numeric/taxonomic rules collapsed across 8 surfaces into a single `content/audit-contract.md` canonical: voice-pass threshold (≥85), audit-tier mapping (polished ≥85, solid 70–84, rough <70), max-revision rounds (3, applied identically to daily-piece and interactive loops), and the `qualityFlag` taxonomy (closed set: `'low'` on max-fail, otherwise null). Behaviour-preserving — no value changes; rule bodies unchanged from the canonical prose previously trapped in `audit-tier.ts:1-18` header docstring and `director.ts:386-393` comment block.

**The change.** New `content/audit-contract.md` (~70 lines). New SOURCES row in `agents/scripts/codegen-contracts.mjs` produces `AUDIT_CONTRACT` in `agents/src/shared/generated/contracts.ts` (now 29,105 bytes; five exports: VOICE_CONTRACT, INTERACTIVE_HTML_REFERENCE, BEAT_CONTRACT, INTERACTIVE_CONTRACT, AUDIT_CONTRACT). New `agents/src/shared/audit-thresholds.ts` exports the agents-side TS constants (`VOICE_PASS_THRESHOLD = 85`, `TIER_SOLID_FLOOR = 70`, `MAX_AUDIT_ROUNDS = 3`); new `src/lib/audit-thresholds.ts` mirrors the same three constants on the site-worker side, header comment naming the contract canonical + the agents-side file as parallel (same shape as the existing `cadence.ts` ↔ `admin-settings.ts` mirror). Eight code-side edits in six files: `director.ts:23` MAX_REVISIONS becomes an aliased import; `voice-auditor.ts:38` swaps the `>= 85` literal for `VOICE_PASS_THRESHOLD`; `voice-auditor-prompt.ts:26` swaps the `>= 85` JSON-spec literal for `${VOICE_PASS_THRESHOLD}` interpolation; `interactive-generator.ts:38` INTERACTIVE_MAX_ROUNDS becomes an aliased import; `interactive-auditor-prompt.ts:23` `INTERACTIVE_VOICE_MIN_SCORE` becomes `= VOICE_PASS_THRESHOLD` re-export alias; `audit-tier.ts:27-29` swaps both literals for imports and trims the file-header docstring (philosophy now lives in the contract, no longer duplicated).

**Q1–Q9 resolutions.**

1. **Contract scope — wider audit-philosophy.** Numbers-only would lose the *why*; the philosophical narrative was already canonical English in `audit-tier.ts:1-18` and `director.ts:386-393`. The contract now carries: the three gates, the 85/70 threshold reasoning ("daily cadence matters more than perfection — Rough lets the reader calibrate"), the 3-round revision rationale ("ship-or-flag boundary, not a deeper-rewrite opportunity"), the publish-anyway-on-max-fail rule ("a newspaper never skips a day"), and the closed `qualityFlag` taxonomy.
2. **Cross-worker mirror — site-worker keeps its own constants.** Matches the `cadence.ts` ↔ `admin-settings.ts` precedent exactly. New `src/lib/audit-thresholds.ts` carries the same three constants; file-header comment names the contract canonical + the agents-side file as parallel. No new infrastructure (per the brief), no new codegen target on the site side. The `audit-tier.ts:24` `voiceScore == null` fallback already provides drift defence.
3. **MAX_REVISIONS vs INTERACTIVE_MAX_ROUNDS — ONE shared agents-side constant.** `MAX_AUDIT_ROUNDS = 3` exported from the new shared module; both director.ts and interactive-generator.ts import it. The `interactive-generator.ts:36-37` docstring previously said the values "must agree" — encoding that in code makes the design intent the contract. Local aliases preserve in-file readability (`MAX_REVISIONS` in director.ts, `INTERACTIVE_MAX_ROUNDS` in interactive-generator.ts stay as the names readers see at use-sites). If a future session decides interactives should retry differently, that becomes an explicit unalias change rather than a silent drift.
4. **`qualityFlag` taxonomy — split.** Contract body NAMES the taxonomy in plain English (closed set, `'low'` only on max-fail). TS type-union sites stay code-side and self-mirror via the shared `'low' | null` literal — no reasonable single source across two worker packages plus two Astro Zod schemas (`content.config.ts:55, 129`). Type union sites (~8 across `interactive-generator.ts`, `observer.ts`, `audit-tier.ts`, `LessonLayout.astro`) and setter sites (`director.ts:395`, `interactive-generator.ts:724`, `:1105`) and persist sites (`director.ts:483/:514`, `interactive-generator.ts:741/:780/:1135/:1173`) all left as documented exceptions — the `'low'` literal at those sites IS the rule application, not duplication of the rule body.
5. **System-vs-user split — Tier-2 audit-context paraphrase.** `voice-auditor-prompt.ts:26` is a system prompt; the `>= 85` JSON-spec literal is response-format spec, not rule body. Swap the literal for `${VOICE_PASS_THRESHOLD}` interpolation; do NOT inject `${AUDIT_CONTRACT}` here — the auditor already gets `${VOICE_CONTRACT}`, and the threshold is response-shape contract, not voice rule body. Mirrors beats Q6 ("swap the bytes, keep the structure").
6. **No prompt currently injects `${AUDIT_CONTRACT}` at runtime.** Director isn't a prompt (zero LLM calls); voice-auditor-prompt + interactive-auditor-prompt get the values via named TS constants. The contract is canonical narrative for human readers (developers, operators, future agents). Codegen still produces `AUDIT_CONTRACT` for canonical-source consistency and so future prompt sites (Integrator's revise prompt, a future audit-summariser) can opt-in via `${...}` without redesign. The verifier-fresh check still catches drift on the canonical markdown. This is a deliberate divergence from the prior pattern (voice/beats/interactive are all `${...}`-injected) — flagged here so a future reader doesn't think it was missed.
7. **`INTERACTIVE_VOICE_MIN_SCORE` — kept as alias.** `export const INTERACTIVE_VOICE_MIN_SCORE = VOICE_PASS_THRESHOLD;` documents the intentional value-share at the import point and reads cleanly in the per-dimension scoring lines below. The HTML 75/75/75 constants at `interactive-auditor-prompt.ts:30-32` are a different rule cluster (HTML structure/essence/factual thresholds) and not in this scope.
8. **Names.** `content/audit-contract.md` (mirrors voice/beat/interactive). `AUDIT_CONTRACT` codegen export. `agents/src/shared/audit-thresholds.ts` (file name describes the constants, parallel to `admin-settings.ts`). `src/lib/audit-thresholds.ts` re-uses the same three names verbatim.
9. **Book + glossary.** `book/11-quality-gates.md` line 13 already cites the thresholds; added a contract pointer in the same style as chapter 09's beat-contract pointer. `book/09-the-sixteen-roles.md` Voice Auditor entry gets an audit-contract pointer parallel to the beat / interactive contract pointers added in prior sessions; Director and Integrator entries similarly. `book/12-publishing.md` qualityFlag mention gets a contract pointer (the publish-anyway philosophy is now in the contract, not just inline in the chapter). `book/99-glossary.md` gains a new alphabetical "Audit contract" entry between "Astro" and "Auditor"; existing "Codegen" entry updated (count grows to five sources, add audit-contract.md to the list); existing "Polished / Solid / Rough" entry gets a contract pointer.

**Verification.** `pnpm codegen` ✓ (29,105 bytes), `pnpm verify-contracts-fresh` ✓, full `pnpm verify-*` suite to be run before commit, manual greps to confirm zero remaining `>= 85` / `>= 70` / `MAX_REVISIONS = 3` / `INTERACTIVE_MAX_ROUNDS = 3` outside the canonical sources.

**Forward observation.** Lower risk than prior session content shifts — this extraction touches code-side constants (numeric value-replacements) more than prompt content. The voice-auditor-prompt's `${VOICE_PASS_THRESHOLD}` interpolation produces a byte-identical prompt body (the literal `85` becomes interpolated `85`), so Claude's voice-scoring should be unchanged. The audit-tier.ts docstring trim does not affect runtime. If any signal shifts materially on the next 3–5 cron pieces, revert via `git revert` on the extraction commit.

**What's NOT changed.** No rule values modified. The InteractiveGenerator JSON-quote-drop wobble (`[observing] 2026-05-03`) remains deferred until Foundation Fix completes. The HTML auditor 75/75/75 thresholds are a different rule cluster (interactive shape, not audit-tier) and not in scope. The qualityFlag taxonomy is not widened beyond `'low' | null`. No shared cross-worker package introduced. Subsequent Task 02 sessions: fact-check, curator, audio, categoriser.

**Branch:** `foundation-fix-02-extraction-audit-thresholds` off `75c008f` (the quiz branch tip; the quiz PR hadn't merged to main at session start, so this branches off the quiz work directly to keep iterative work building cleanly. Eventual merge order is beats PR → main → quiz PR rebased on top → main → audit-thresholds PR rebased on top).
